front: Film: peasants hire seven warriors to defend their village from bandits; ends with a samurai saying 'The farmers have won, not us' after two samurai die to musket fire in the rain
Film: remade as The Magnificent Seven (1960, John Sturges, starring Yul Brynner) and also inspired A Fistful of Dollars
Film: two bickering peasant framing characters were adapted by George Lucas as C-3PO and R2-D2; story elements about saving a princess also used in Star Wars
Film: various characters give conflicting testimonies about a samurai's murder and his wife's rape; ends with a woodcutter taking in an abandoned baby
Film: said to be the first film to aim the camera directly at the sun; Toshiro Mifune played the bandit Tajomaru; woodcutter played by Takashi Shimura
Film: King Lear adaptation in which warlord Hidetora Ichimonji divides his kingdom among three sons; the blind peasant Tsurumaru is the only one to escape the carnage
Film: ends with a blood-orange sky and a blind man (Tsurumaru) standing atop ruins; the court jester Kyoami appears; we never see a close-up of Lady Sue's face
Film: Toru Takemitsu's score includes 'Flute of Darkness' (shakuhachi over burning buildings) and 'Hell's Picture Scroll'
Film: bureaucrat secretly diagnosed with stomach cancer (told it's a 'mild ulcer') devotes remaining months to building a playground in a slum; ends singing 'The Gondola Song' on a snowy swing set
Film: protagonist's character arc inspired by Ivan Ilyich from Tolstoy's The Death of Ivan Ilyich
Film: trademark horizontal wipe transitions link a series of bureaucrats who pass women between departments in a circular referral process
Film: adapted from Ed McBain's King's Ransom; kidnappers accidentally abduct a shoe executive's chauffeur's son instead of the executive's own son; Toshiro Mifune plays executive Kingo Gondo
Film: the only color shot in an otherwise black-and-white climax is pink smoke above a skyline; a rigged briefcase is thrown from a bathroom window during a train sequence
Film: final confrontation has the protagonist and villain separated by wire mesh and reflective glass — their faces superimpose each other when either speaks
Film: a bodyguard pits two rival criminal families against each other; inspired by Dashiell Hammett's Red Harvest; Sergio Leone remade it without permission as A Fistful of Dollars
Film: also remade without permission as Django (1966), starring Franco Nero as a former Union soldier who carries a coffin containing a machine gun
Film: Macbeth adaptation set in feudal Japan; Toshiro Mifune plays the Macbeth analogue; influenced George Lucas alongside Ran
Film: first of Kurosawa's sixteen collaborations with Toshiro Mifune; an alcoholic doctor bonds with a yakuza gangster who has tuberculosis; Kurosawa used the score to evade censors
Film: Palme d'Or-winning film in which a thief saved from crucifixion impersonates the deceased warlord Shingen; funded with help from George Lucas

Film: anthology with segments 'The Peach Orchard' and 'Crows'; in 'Crows,' an art student hears Chopin's Raindrop Prelude and enters Van Gogh's Wheatfield with Crows, meeting a Martin Scorsese-portrayed Van Gogh

Film: Kurosawa's only 70mm film; made for Soviet studio Mosfilm; features a shot of the moon and setting sun in opposite corners of the frame while the title hunter says the sun is the most important person

Director: wrote the memoir Something Like an Autobiography; one chapter traces a bathtub sense-memory to the Dreams 'Crows' segment; another recounts a failed filming attempt at the summit of Mt. Fuji
Director: one of the three great directors of Golden Age Japanese cinema, alongside Yasujiro Ozu and Kenji Mizoguchi
Director: George Lucas described his approach to set design with the phrase 'immaculate reality' (rules established must be maintained); Lucas helped fund Kagemusha after Star Wars succeeded
Director: trademark use of horizontal wipe transitions, also borrowed by George Lucas for Star Wars
Director: was the first choice to direct the 1964 Tokyo Olympics documentary but was replaced by Kon Ichikawa after a dispute with producers
